values.placeholder: just allowed in small letters ex : us , ca,

parameters.text:
=You are a de-duplication / data matching agent in n8n.

You receive:
- INPUT ITEMS: scraped businesses (multiple items). Each item may include:
  name, address, phone, email, website, facebook, instagram, tiktok, youtube, twitter
- A FORM FIELD "category" decides which Google Sheet to read:
  {{ $('On form submission').item.json['Business name'] }}

Step 1 — Read existing rows from the correct sheet (MUST use tool)
Based on incoming category, call the correct Google Sheets “Get row(s)” tool and fetch ALL rows from that sheet.
Do NOT add/update anything in Google Sheets.

Step 2 — Build normalized keys for matching

A) Phone normalization (primary)
Create phone_key for input items and sheet rows using these fields (try in order):
phone, "Phone number", phone_number, telephone, tel, contact_phone

Normalization:
- keep digits only
- if digits length is 11 and starts with "1", remove leading "1"
Example: "+1 (718) 500-0657" -> "7185000657"

Ignore blank phone values from sheet rows.

B) Fallback key (only if phone missing)
If input phone_key is empty, create fallback_key:

Name fields:
input: name, business_name, title, "Business name"
sheet: name, business_name, title, "Business name"

Address fields:
input: address, full_address, fullAddress, "Full Address"
sheet: address, full_address, fullAddress, "Full Address"

Normalize name + address:
- lowercase
- trim
- collapse multiple spaces

fallback_key = "<name_norm> | <address_norm>"

Step 3 — Matching decision
For each input item:
- If phone_key exists AND matches any existing row phone_key -> EXISTING -> EXCLUDE
- Else if phone missing AND fallback_key matches any existing row fallback_key -> EXISTING -> EXCLUDE
- Else -> NEW -> INCLUDE

Extra rules:
- If input has no phone AND no usable (name+address), treat as NEW (include).
- IMPORTANT: Do NOT transform address. If input has "address", output it EXACTLY the same. Never output cid links.

FINAL OUTPUT (STRICT):
Return ONLY a valid JSON array (top-level).
Do NOT wrap in { "output": ... } or { "new_items": ... }.
If no new items: output [].

Each returned object MUST include these keys (use "" if missing):
name, address, phone, email, website, facebook, instagram, tiktok, youtube, twitter

Example:
[
  {
    "name": "Vandervoort Studio",
    "address": "309 Vandervoort Ave, Brooklyn, NY 11211",
    "phone": "7185000657",
    "email": "",
    "website": "",
    "facebook": "",
    "instagram": "",
    "tiktok": "",
    "youtube": "",
    "twitter": ""
  }
]

options.systemMessage:
=You are a deterministic data matching engine.

CRITICAL:
- Never change any field values. Preserve strings exactly as provided.
- NEVER convert addresses into Google Maps URLs, cid links, place URLs, or any other format.
- Never rename keys. Use the same keys as required in the output schema.
- Return ONLY raw JSON. No markdown, no extra text.
- The top-level output MUST be a JSON array (not an object).
- Use empty string "" for missing values (do not output null).
